rules: 专有名词是特定人、地点、事物或组织的名称，首字母必须大写。如：China, Beijing, Monday, January, Tom
由普通名词构成的专有名词前通常加定冠词 the。如：the Great Wall（长城）, the United States（美国）, the People's Republic of China
单独的人名、地名、国名一般不加冠词。如：John, London, France（但复合国名要加 the：the United Kingdom）
江河、海洋、山脉、群岛等地理名称前必须加 the。如：the Yangtze River, the Pacific Ocean, the Alps, the Philippines
组织机构、报刊杂志、船舶、乐器等专有名称前常加 the。如：the United Nations, the New York Times, the Titanic, the piano
节假日名称通常不加冠词。如：Christmas, New Year's Day, National Day（但 the Spring Festival 习惯加 the）
examples: Beijing is the capital of China.（北京是中国的首都）
the Great Wall is one of the seven wonders of the world.（长城是世界七大奇迹之一）
The Yangtze River is the longest river in Asia.（长江是亚洲最长的河流）
Mr. Smith comes from the United Kingdom.（史密斯先生来自英国）
common_mistakes: monday → Monday（星期首字母必须大写）
the beijing → Beijing（单个城市名不加 the）
great wall → the Great Wall（复合专有名词必须加 the）
the Japan → Japan（单个国名不加 the，但 the United States 要加）

rules: 可数名词有单数和复数两种形式。单数前可用 a/an，复数通常在词尾加 -s 或 -es。
规则复数变化：①一般加 -s（book→books）；②以 s/x/sh/ch 结尾加 -es（bus→buses）；③辅音+y 结尾变 y 为 i 加 -es（city→cities）；④以 f/fe 结尾变 f 为 v 加 -es（leaf→leaves）；⑤以 o 结尾，有生命加 -es（potato→potatoes），无生命加 -s（photo→photos）
不规则复数变化：man→men, woman→women, child→children, tooth→teeth, foot→feet, mouse→mice
单复数同形：sheep, deer, fish（同种鱼）, Chinese, Japanese
复合名词变复数：①一般只变中心词（apple tree→apple trees）；②man/woman 修饰时两部分都变（woman doctor→women doctors）；③无中心词时在末尾加 s（grown-up→grown-ups）
只能用复数的名词：trousers, glasses, scissors, clothes, people, police, cattle（这些词作主语时谓语用复数）
examples: There are three apples on the table.（桌上有三个苹果）
Many countries took part in the meeting.（许多国家参加了会议）
Women doctors are very popular in this hospital.（女医生在这家医院很受欢迎）
The police are searching for the thief.（警察正在搜捕小偷）
common_mistakes: sheeps → sheep（单复数同形，不加 s）
childs → children（不规则变化）
woman doctors → women doctors（两部分都变复数）
potatos → potatoes（有生命的 o 结尾加 es）

rules: 不可数名词没有复数形式，不能加 s，也不能用 a/an 修饰。表示数量时用 some, much, a little, a lot of 等。
常见不可数名词：information, advice, furniture, weather, news, money, bread, rice, milk, water, paper, work, homework, housework, knowledge, equipment, luggage, traffic, music, progress
要表示不可数名词的具体数量，需借助量词：a piece of advice/information/news, a loaf of bread, a bottle of water, a sheet of paper
有些名词既是可数又是不可数，但含义不同：glass（玻璃，不可数）/ a glass（玻璃杯，可数）；paper（纸，不可数）/ a paper（报纸/论文，可数）；experience（经验，不可数）/ an experience（经历，可数）
不可数名词作主语时，谓语动词用单数。Information is important.
用 much, (a) little, a great deal of, a large amount of 修饰不可数名词；用 many, (a) few, a large number of 修饰可数名词
examples: Could you give me some advice?（你能给我一些建议吗？）
There is little furniture in the room.（房间里几乎没有什么家具）
What fine weather we are having!（多好的天气啊！）
I need a piece of paper to write on.（我需要一张纸来写）
common_mistakes: a good news → a piece of good news 或 good news（news 不可数）
many furniture → much furniture（furniture 不可数）
informations → information（不加 s）
a bread → a piece of bread 或 some bread（bread 不可数）

rules: 名词所有格表示"……的"。有 's 所有格和 of 所有格两种形式。
's 所有格主要用于有生命的名词：①单数加 's（Tom's book）；②以 s 结尾的复数加 '（the students' books）；③不规则复数加 's（children's toys）
of 所有格主要用于无生命的名词：the title of the book, the capital of China
双重所有格（of + 's 所有格/名词性物主代词）：a friend of my father's = one of my father's friends
时间、距离、重量、国家、城市等无生命名词也可用 's 所有格：today's newspaper, ten minutes' walk, China's history
并列所有格：①A and B's + 单数 = 共同拥有（Tom and Jerry's room 共住的房间）；②A's and B's + 复数 = 各自拥有（Tom's and Jerry's rooms 各自的房间）
examples: This is Tom's bike.（这是汤姆的自行车）
It's twenty minutes' walk from my home to school.（从我家到学校步行20分钟）
He is a friend of my brother's.（他是我哥哥的一个朋友）
Tom's and Peter's fathers are both teachers.（汤姆的爸爸和彼得的爸爸都是老师）
common_mistakes: Toms book → Tom's book（漏掉撇号）
the students's books → the students' books（s 结尾复数只加 '）
a friend of my brother → a friend of my brother's（双重所有格必须加 's）

rules: 集合名词表示一群人或物的总称。常见的：family, team, class, committee, audience, crowd, government, staff, crew, public
集合名词作主语时，强调整体概念用单数谓语，强调个体成员用复数谓语。如：My family is a big one.（整体）/ My family are watching TV.（个体）
有些集合名词总是用复数谓语：people, police, cattle, clergy, militia
有些集合名词总是用单数谓语：machinery, equipment, furniture, luggage, clothing, poetry
判断标准：看说话人是把集合体当作一个整体还是当作多个个体。有 each/all 等强调个体的词时用复数；有 every/whole 等词时用单数
examples: The class is very big.（这个班很大——整体）
The class are doing experiments.（班上同学在做实验——个体）
The police are looking for the lost child.（警察在找失踪的孩子——总是复数）
My family were having dinner when he arrived.（他到时我们全家在吃饭——个体）
common_mistakes: The police is coming → The police are coming（police 总是复数）
The team are a strong one → The team is a strong one（整体用单数）

rules: 主谓一致的"语法一致"原则：谓语动词在人称和数上与主语保持一致。这是最基本的原则。
单数主语 + 单数谓语：He is a student. / The book was interesting.
复数主语 + 复数谓语：They are students. / The books were interesting.
由 and 连接的两个名词作主语时谓语用复数：Tom and Mary are friends.
特殊情况：①each/every + 单数名词 + and + each/every + 单数名词 → 单数谓语（Every boy and every girl has a book.）；②many a + 单数名词 → 单数谓语（Many a student has tried.）；③more than one + 单数名词 → 单数谓语；④不定式、动名词、主语从句作主语 → 单数谓语
no/each/every/many a + 单数名词 + and + no/each/every/many a + 单数名词 → 单数谓语
"a series/species/means + of"作主语时谓语用单数
examples: To say something is one thing, to do it is another.（说是一回事，做是另一回事）
Many a student has made this mistake.（许多学生犯过这个错误）
Every teacher and every student was invited.（每个老师和学生都被邀请了）
Writing stories and poems is what I enjoy most.（写故事和诗歌是我最喜欢的）
common_mistakes: Many a students have tried → Many a student has tried（单数谓语）
Every boy and girl have → Every boy and every girl has（单数谓语）

rules: 就近原则：谓语动词的人称和数与最靠近的主语保持一致。
either...or...：Either you or he is wrong.（谓语与 he 一致）
neither...nor...：Neither he nor I am interested.（谓语与 I 一致）
not only...but also...：Not only the students but also the teacher was invited.
not...but...：Not you but I am to blame.
There be 句型：be 动词与最靠近的名词保持一致。There is a book and two pens on the desk.
Here be 句型同理：Here is a pen and some paper for you.
or, whether...or... 连接的主语也遵循就近原则
examples: There is a desk, a bed and some chairs in the room.
Either your teacher or you are mistaken.
Neither Jane nor her brothers need a consent.
Not only I but also Tom and Mary are fond of music.
common_mistakes: There are a book and two pens → There is a book and two pens（就近 a book 单数）
Neither he nor I is → Neither he nor I am（就近 I→am）

rules: 意义一致原则：谓语动词根据主语的实际含义（而非语法形式）确定单复数。
表示时间、距离、金钱、重量等的复数名词作主语时，视为整体，谓语用单数：Twenty years is a long time. / Five thousand dollars is enough.
以 -ics 结尾的学科名称作主语时，谓语用单数：Mathematics is difficult. / Physics was my favorite subject.
以 s 结尾的国名、机构名、书名、报纸名作主语时，谓语用单数：The United States is a powerful country. / The New York Times is widely read.
群岛、山脉、瀑布等作主语时，谓语通常用复数：The Philippines are in the Pacific Ocean. / The Alps are in Europe.
"the + 形容词"指一类人时谓语用复数：The poor are getting poorer. / The young are full of energy.
分数/百分数 + of + 名词 → 谓语与名词一致：Two thirds of the water is polluted. / Two thirds of the students are present.
all/most/some/half/part/the rest + of + 名词 → 谓语与名词一致
examples: Ten miles is a long distance.（十英里是很长的距离）
Mathematics is my favorite subject.（数学是我最喜欢的科目）
The young are the future of our country.（年轻人是我们国家的未来）
Three fourths of the surface is covered with water.（四分之三的表面被水覆盖）
common_mistakes: Twenty dollars are enough → Twenty dollars is enough（金钱整体单数）
Two thirds of the apple were eaten → Two thirds of the apple was eaten（apple 单数）

rules: 人称代词分为主格和宾格。主格作主语（I, he, she, we, they, you, it），宾格作宾语（me, him, her, us, them, you, it）。
动词后接宾格：Please help me. / I saw him yesterday.
介词后接宾格：This is for her. / Come with us.
主语位置必须用主格：He and I are good friends.（不是 Him and me）
人称代词的排列顺序：单数通常是 you → he/she → I（第二、第三、第一人称），复数通常是 we → you → they。
在承认错误时，第一人称放在前面：I and he are to blame.
it 作无人称主语：表示天气、时间、距离等。It is raining. / It is late. / It is five miles to the station.
examples: She and I went to the park together.（她和我一起去公园）
Please give the book to me.（请把书给我）
It was very kind of you to help us.（你帮助我们真是太好了）
I don't think it will rain tomorrow.（我觉得明天不会下雨）
common_mistakes: Me and Tom went → Tom and I went（主语用主格）
between you and I → between you and me（介词后用宾格）
Let we go → Let us go（let 后用宾格）

rules: 反身代词表示"某人自己"。myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves
用于宾语和主语是同一人或物时：He hurt himself.（他伤了自己）
用于强调：I saw it myself.（我亲眼看见的）
固定搭配：enjoy oneself（玩得开心）, help oneself（自便/自取）, by oneself（独自）, teach oneself（自学）, behave oneself（规矩点）, make oneself at home（别客气）
反身代词必须与主语在人称、数上保持一致。单数 yourself，复数 yourselves
examples: Help yourself to some cake.（请随便吃蛋糕）
They enjoyed themselves at the party.（他们在聚会上玩得很开心）
She taught herself English.（她自学英语）
Did you do it all by yourself?（你一个人做的吗？）
common_mistakes: Help you to some cake → Help yourself（反身代词）
They enjoyed them → They enjoyed themselves（反身代词）
He taught hisself English → He taught himself English（himself 才是正确形式）

rules: 不定代词指不明确指代对象的名词。主要有：some/any/no/every + thing/one/body, all, each, both, either, neither, none, few, little, many, much, other, another, one
复合不定代词(something, anything, nothing, everything 等)作主语时谓语用单数。Nothing is impossible.
形容词修饰复合不定代词时必须后置：something important, nothing special, anyone else
some-/any- 的区别：some- 用于肯定句，any- 用于否定句、疑问句和条件句。在请求、建议的疑问句中用 some-（期望肯定回答）
both/either/neither 用于两者：both（两者都）, either（两者任一）, neither（两者都不）
all/none 用于三者及以上：all（都）, none（都不）
few/a few + 可数复数；little/a little + 不可数。有 a 表示肯定（有几个/一点），无 a 表示否定（几乎没有）
each 强调个体，every 强调整体。each of + 复数 + 单数谓语
other/the other/others/the others/another 的区别要掌握
examples: Is there anything wrong with your car?（你的车有什么毛病吗？）
Few people can answer this question.（几乎没人能回答这个问题）
Neither of the two answers is correct.（两个答案都不对）
Some are singing, others are dancing.（有些在唱歌，另一些在跳舞）
common_mistakes: important something → something important（形容词后置）
anybody are → anybody is（复合不定代词单数）
few 和 a few 混淆：few = 几乎没（否定），a few = 有几个（肯定）

rules: 指示代词 this/these（近指）和 that/those（远指）
this/that 指单数，these/those 指复数
电话用语：This is...（我是...）；Who is that?（你是谁？）
that/those 可代替前面提到的名词避免重复：The weather in Beijing is colder than that in Shanghai
examples: This is my book and that is yours.
The cars made in Japan are better than those made in China.
common_mistakes: this are → this is
that 代替不可数/单数，those 代替复数

rules: it 作代词指代上文提到的事物：I have a dog. It is very cute.
it 作无人称主语：表天气(It is raining)、时间(It is late)、距离(It is far)
it 作形式主语：It is important to learn English.
it 作形式宾语：I find it difficult to understand.
强调句型：It is/was...that/who...
examples: It is never too late to learn.
I think it necessary to tell him the truth.
It was Tom who broke the window.
common_mistakes: 用 this/that 代替 it 作形式主语
强调句中误用 which 代替 that

rules: 基数词表示数量。1-12有独立形式，13-19以-teen结尾，20-90整十以-ty结尾。
hundred, thousand, million, billion前有具体数字时不加s：two hundred, five thousand
hundreds/thousands/millions of 表示"成百上千/成千上万"时加s：hundreds of people
几十几的表达：十位和个位间加连字符：twenty-one, ninety-nine
"数词+名词"作定语时，名词用单数：a five-year-old boy, a ten-mile walk
examples: There are three hundred students in our school.
Hundreds of birds are flying south.
She is a sixteen-year-old girl.
common_mistakes: two hundreds → two hundred（具体数字不加s）
five-years-old → five-year-old（作定语时名词单数）

rules: 序数词表示顺序。1-3: first, second, third；4-19: 基数词+th（注意 fifth, eighth, ninth, twelfth 的特殊拼写）；20以上整十：变 y 为 ieth（twenty→twentieth）
序数词前通常加 the：the first time, the second floor
a + 序数词 = 又一/再一：a second chance = 又一次机会
examples: He lives on the twelfth floor.
This is the third time I have been here.
They gave him a second chance.
common_mistakes: twelveth → twelfth（注意拼写）
nineth → ninth（去e加th）

rules: 分数表达：分子用基数词，分母用序数词。分子>1时分母加 s。
1/2 = a half / one half；1/4 = a quarter / one quarter；3/4 = three quarters / three fourths
百分数：数字 + percent。percent of + 名词 → 谓语与名词一致。
小数：整数部分按基数词读，小数点读 point，小数部分逐位读。
examples: Two thirds of the earth is covered with water.
More than 40 percent of the students wear glasses.
Zero point two five equals a quarter.
common_mistakes: three-fifth → three-fifths（分子>1分母加s）
percent of 后用错谓语单复数

rules: 年份读法：1998→nineteen ninety-eight；2000→two thousand
日期写法：June 1st/June 1/1st June；读法：June the first
时刻表达：整点→...o'clock；半点→half past...；分钟→...past/to
in one's twenties/thirties=在某人20/30多岁时
examples: He was born on January 27, 1998.
She is in her early twenties.
common_mistakes: on 1998 → in 1998（年份用 in）
in Monday → on Monday（具体某天用 on）

rules: 约数表达：about/around/approximately + 数字
hundreds/thousands/millions of = 成百上千
dozens of = 几十；scores of = 许多
several + 名词 = 几个
some + 数字 = 大约：some fifty people = 大约50人
examples: Hundreds of people attended the concert.
There are dozens of books on the shelf.
common_mistakes: two hundreds → two hundred（具体数字不加s）
hundred of → hundreds of（表约数加s和of）

rules: 不定冠词 a/an 用于单数可数名词前，表示泛指"一个"。
a 用于辅音音素开头的词前：a book, a university（/juː/）, a European（/jʊ/）
an 用于元音音素开头的词前：an apple, an hour（/aʊ/）, an honest man（/ɒ/），an MP3（/em/）
关键规则：看发音（音素），不看字母！a useful tool（/j/辅音），an umbrella（/ʌ/元音）
examples: I have a useful book.（我有一本有用的书）
He is an honest boy.（他是个诚实的孩子）
She waited for me for an hour.（她等了我一小时）
common_mistakes: a apple → an apple（元音音素）
an university → a university（/juː/辅音音素）

rules: 定冠词 the 表示特指——说话双方都知道的人或物。
必须用 the 的情况：①独一无二的事物（the sun, the moon）；②序数词和最高级前（the first, the best）；③演奏乐器（play the piano）；④江河海洋山脉（the Nile）；⑤上文提过的人或物
the + 形容词 = 一类人：the rich（富人）, the poor（穷人）, the young（年轻人）
examples: The earth moves around the sun.
He is the tallest boy in our class.
She plays the violin very well.
common_mistakes: sun rises in east → The sun rises in the east（独一无二+方位）
play piano → play the piano（乐器加the）

rules: 零冠词指不用冠词的情况。
不用冠词：①球类运动（play basketball）；②一日三餐（have breakfast）；③交通工具（by bus）；④学科名称（math, physics）；⑤节假日（Christmas, New Year's Day）；⑥称呼语（Come in, Doctor）；⑦泛指的复数名词和不可数名词
固定短语中的零冠词：go to bed, go to school, at home, in hospital, in prison, at work, by chance, on foot
examples: We have lunch at twelve every day.
He goes to school by bus.
Children like playing football.
common_mistakes: play the basketball → play basketball（球类零冠词）
have a breakfast → have breakfast（三餐零冠词）

rules: 固定短语中冠词不可省略：in the morning, at the moment, all the time
the + 比较级, the + 比较级 = 越...越...
at the same time, on the other hand, in the end, by the way
have a cold, have a fever, have a headache（疾病前用 a）
make a decision, take a walk, have a look（固定搭配中 a）
examples: The more you read, the more you know.
He is in the habit of getting up early.
common_mistakes: in morning → in the morning
at same time → at the same time

rules: 序数词前加 a 表示"又一"：a second chance
most 前不加 the 表示"大多数"：most people
抽象名词具体化时可加 a：a success（一个成功的人/事）, a pleasure（一件乐事）
物质名词具体化：a coffee（一杯咖啡）, a beer（一杯啤酒）
examples: He tried a second time and succeeded.
Most students like sports.
common_mistakes: most of students → most students（泛指不加 the）
a second 和 the second 混淆

rules: 形容词作定语修饰名词：a beautiful girl
形容词作表语（系动词后）：She looks happy.
常见的系动词：be, become, get, turn, grow, look, sound, smell, taste, feel, seem, appear, keep, stay
形容词作宾语补足语：make me happy, find it interesting
多个形容词排列顺序：限定词→观点→大小→形状→年龄→颜色→来源→材料→用途+名词
examples: The food tastes delicious.
We found the movie very interesting.
common_mistakes: She looks happily → She looks happy（系动词+形容词）
用副词修饰系动词：feel badly → feel bad

rules: 比较级用于两者之间的比较。结构：A + be + 比较级 + than + B
变化规则：①单音节词 + er（tall→taller）；②以 e 结尾 + r（large→larger）；③辅音+y 结尾变 y 为 i+er（happy→happier）；④重读闭音节双写辅音+er（big→bigger）；⑤多音节词前加 more（beautiful→more beautiful）
不规则变化：good→better, bad→worse, many/much→more, little→less, far→farther/further, old→older/elder
much/far/even/still/a lot/a bit + 比较级表示"……得多/稍微"
比较级 + and + 比较级 = 越来越……（better and better）
the + 比较级, the + 比较级 = 越……越……（The more, the better）
比较对象要对等：His bag is bigger than mine (my bag).
examples: Tom is taller than his brother.
This book is much more interesting than that one.
The harder you work, the better result you will get.
common_mistakes: more taller → much taller（不能用 more 修饰比较级）
He is taller than me → He is taller than I (am)（正式语法）

rules: 最高级用于三者或以上的比较。结构：the + 最高级 + 范围
变化规则：①单音节词 + est（tall→tallest）；②多音节词前加 most（beautiful→the most beautiful）
不规则变化：good→best, bad→worst, many→most, little→least, far→farthest/furthest
常用范围状语：in the world, of all, in our class, among..., one of the + 最高级 + 复数名词
examples: She is the most beautiful girl in our class.
This is one of the best movies I have ever seen.
He runs (the) fastest of all.（副词最高级前 the 可省略）
common_mistakes: the most tallest → the tallest（最高级不用 most+est）
漏掉 the：He is tallest in class → He is the tallest in the class

rules: 多个形容词修饰名词时的排列口诀：美小圆旧黄，法国木书房
完整顺序：限定词(a/the/my)→观点(beautiful)→大小(big)→形状(round)→新旧(old)→颜色(red)→来源(Chinese)→材料(wooden)→用途(writing)+名词
同类形容词用逗号分开，不同类不用逗号
examples: a beautiful big round old red Chinese wooden writing table
She has a lovely small white dog.
common_mistakes: a red big apple → a big red apple（大小在颜色前）

rules: -ing 形容词修饰物，表示"令人...的"：interesting, exciting, boring, surprising
-ed 形容词修饰人，表示"感到...的"：interested, excited, bored, surprised

常见易混对：interesting/interested, exciting/excited, boring/bored, surprising/surprised, tiring/tired, frightening/frightened, pleasing/pleased, worrying/worried, amazing/amazed, disappointing/disappointed
以 -ly 结尾但实际是形容词：friendly, lovely, lively, lonely, likely, ugly, silly, elderly
examples: The movie is interesting. I am interested in it.
She gave me a friendly smile.
common_mistakes: I am interesting → I am interested（人用-ed）
She sings lovely → She sings beautifully（lovely是形容词）

rules: 副词通常由形容词加 -ly 构成：quick→quickly, careful→carefully
以辅音+y 结尾变 y 为 i+ly：happy→happily, easy→easily
以 le 结尾去 e 加 y：simple→simply, gentle→gently
以 ic 结尾加 ally：basic→basically, scientific→scientifically
有些词本身既是形容词又是副词：fast, hard, early, late, high, low, deep, near
注意：hard(努力)≠hardly(几乎不)；late(晚)≠lately(最近)；near(近)≠nearly(几乎)
examples: She sings beautifully.
He works hard every day.
common_mistakes: She sings beautiful → She sings beautifully
He works hardly → He works hard（hardly=几乎不）

rules: 副词比较级和最高级的构成：单音节加 er/est（fast→faster→fastest），多音节前加 more/most（quickly→more quickly→most quickly）
不规则变化：well→better→best, badly→worse→worst, much→more→most, little→less→least, far→farther/further→farthest/furthest
比较级+than，最高级+范围。副词最高级前的 the 可省略
examples: He runs faster than me.
She sings (the) best of all.
common_mistakes: more faster → faster（不能用 more+er）

rules: 频度副词表示动作发生的频率：always(100%)、usually(90%)、often(70%)、sometimes(50%)、seldom(20%)、rarely(10%)、never(0%)
频度副词位置：be 动词后、实义动词前、助动词和实义动词之间
how often 提问频率
examples: He is always late.
She never eats meat.
How often do you exercise?
common_mistakes: He goes always → He always goes（实义动词前）
He never is late → He is never late（be 动词后）

rules: hard（努力地）/ hardly（几乎不）：He works hard. / He hardly works.
late（晚）/ lately（最近）：He came late. / Have you seen him lately?
near（近）/ nearly（几乎）：He lives near. / He nearly fell.
high（高）/ highly（高度地/非常）：fly high / think highly of
deep（深）/ deeply（深深地）：dig deep / deeply moved
close（近）/ closely（仔细地）：come close / watch closely
wide（宽）/ widely（广泛地）：open wide / widely used
free（免费）/ freely（自由地）：get in free / speak freely
examples: He hardly ever goes out.（他几乎不出门）
The book is widely read.（这本书被广泛阅读）
common_mistakes: I can't hardly → I can hardly（hardly 本身已是否定）

rules: 副词在句中的位置：①方式副词通常在动词后或宾语后；②地点副词在方式副词后、时间副词前；③频度副词在 be 后实义前；④句子副词（fortunately, honestly）通常在句首
多个副词顺序：方式→地点→时间
examples: She sang beautifully at the concert last night.
Fortunately, no one was hurt.
common_mistakes: 次序错误：last night at the concert → at the concert last night

rules: 否定副词：hardly, scarcely, barely, seldom, rarely, never, little, not
否定副词置于句首时，句子要用部分倒装：Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset.
hardly...when / no sooner...than / scarcely...when = 一...就...
部分否定：not always, not completely, not all
examples: Seldom does he go to the cinema.
Hardly had he arrived when it began to rain.
common_mistakes: Hardly he had → Hardly had he（倒装）
I haven't never → I have never（双重否定错误）

rules: at + 时刻/正午/夜：at 7:00, at noon, at night, at midnight
on + 具体日期/星期/节日的某天：on Monday, on June 1st, on Christmas Day
in + 月份/季节/年/世纪：in May, in summer, in 2020, in the 21st century
in + 时间段（将来时）：in two days（两天后）
for + 时间段（持续多久）：for two hours
since + 时间点（自从）：since 2010
during + 时间段（在...期间）：during the summer
by + 时间 = 到...为止：by the end of
examples: We have lived here since 2015.
He will be back in an hour.
common_mistakes: on May → in May（月份用 in）
in Sunday → on Sunday（星期用 on）
since two years → for two years（since+时间点）

rules: at + 小地点/点：at the door, at the bus stop, at school
in + 大地点/空间：in Beijing, in the room, in the park
on + 表面/线：on the desk, on the wall, on the river
above/over 在...上方；below/under 在...下方
between 两者之间；among 三者及以上之间
in front of 在...前面（外部）；in the front of 在...前部（内部）
behind 在...后面；beside/next to 在...旁边；near 在...附近；opposite 在...对面
examples: There is a picture on the wall.
He is standing between Tom and Jerry.
common_mistakes: arrive at London → arrive in London（大城市用 in）
between three → among three（三者用 among）

rules: 介词短语 = 介词 + 名词/代词/动名词，在句中作定语、状语或表语
常见动词+介词搭配：agree with, belong to, listen to, wait for, look at/for/after, depend on, think about, care about, talk about, hear from, arrive at/in, pay for, search for, laugh at

常见形容词+介词搭配：good at, interested in, proud of, afraid of, full of, tired of, satisfied with, pleased with, angry with, busy with, different from, absent from, famous for, ready for, thankful to, kind to
常见名词+介词搭配：reason for, answer to, key to, solution to, influence on
examples: He is good at playing chess.
I am looking forward to hearing from you.
common_mistakes: agree sb → agree with sb
belong to sb ✅，be belonged to ❌

rules: across 横穿（从表面穿过）：walk across the street
through 穿过（从内部穿过）：walk through the forest
along 沿着：walk along the river
toward(s) 朝向：walk toward the door
into 进入：jump into the water
onto 到...上面：climb onto the roof
out of 从...出来：get out of the car
from...to... 从...到...：from London to Paris
examples: The train goes through the tunnel.
He jumped into the pool.
common_mistakes: across the forest → through the forest（内部穿过用 through）

rules: besides = 除了...（包括在内）= in addition to；except = 除了...（不包括在内）
except 和 besides 的区别是高频考点
between vs among：between 两者，among 三者及以上
like vs as：like + 名词（像...一样）；as + 从句（像/作为）
with vs by：with+工具（with a pen）；by+方式（by bus）
in vs after：in+时间段（将来时）；after+时间段（过去时）
on the tree（树上长的）vs in the tree（外来物在树上）
examples: Besides English, he also speaks French.
All went except Tom.
common_mistakes: besides 和 except 混淆
arrive to → arrive at/in

rules: 并列连词连接两个对等的语法成分。常见：and, or, but, so, for, yet, nor
and = 和/然后（并列/顺承）：He came in and sat down.
or = 或者/否则（选择）：Hurry up, or you'll be late.
but = 但是（转折）：He is old but strong.
so = 因此/所以（因果）：It rained, so we stayed home.
重要规则：because 和 so 不能同时使用；although 和 but 不能同时使用——这是中英文表达的重大区别
examples: Study hard, and you will succeed.
He is rich, but he is not happy.
common_mistakes: Although he is poor, but he is happy.→ Although he is poor, he is happy.（去掉 but）

rules: 关联连词成对使用：both...and, either...or, neither...nor, not only...but also, whether...or
both...and + 复数谓语
either...or / neither...nor 遵循就近原则
not only...but also 遵循就近原则；not only 在句首时需倒装
whether...or = 无论...还是...
examples: Both Tom and Mary are students.
Not only does he sing, but he also dances.
common_mistakes: Both Tom and Mary is → Both Tom and Mary are
Neither he nor I are → Neither he nor I am（就近I）

rules: 从属连词引导状语从句或名词性从句。
原因：because（语气最强）, since, as（既然/由于）
让步：although, though（虽然）, even though（即使）, while（然而）
条件：if（如果）, unless（除非/如果不）, as long as（只要）
时间：when, while（当...时）, as（一边...一边）, before, after, since, until/till, as soon as（一...就）
目的：so that, in order that（以便）
结果：so...that, such...that（如此...以至于）
比较：than, as...as, not so/as...as
examples: He didn't come because he was ill.
You won't pass unless you study hard.
common_mistakes: because...so 连用
although...but 连用
until 和 not...until 混淆

rules: 时间状语从句的时态规则：主将从现——主句用将来时，从句用一般现在时表将来
when = 当...时（可接短暂/持续动作）：When he comes, I will tell him.
while = 在...期间（接持续动作，常用进行时）：While I was reading, he came in.
as = 一边...一边/随着：As time goes by, we grow older.
as soon as = 一...就...：I will call you as soon as I arrive.
until/till = 直到...：not...until = 直到...才
since = 自从（主句用现在完成时）
before/after = 在...之前/后
examples: I will wait until he comes back.
He has changed a lot since he moved here.
common_mistakes: When he will come → When he comes（从句不用将来时）
He didn't come until I leave → I left（时态一致）

rules: 连接副词表示句子间的逻辑关系：however（然而）, therefore（因此）, moreover/furthermore（此外）, besides（除此之外）, otherwise（否则）, nevertheless（尽管如此）, consequently（因此）, thus（因此）, hence（因此）
连接副词不是连词，不能直接连接两个句子。需要分号或句号隔开，后面加逗号
examples: He was tired; however, he kept working.
It was late. Therefore, we went home.
common_mistakes: He was tired, however he kept working → He was tired; however, he kept working.（逗号连接错误）

rules: 一般现在时：表示习惯性动作、客观真理、现状
形式：主语为第三人称单数时动词加 s/es；其他人称用原形
第三人称单数变化：①一般加 s；②s/x/sh/ch/o 结尾加 es；③辅音+y 变 i 加 es
标志词：every day, usually, often, always, sometimes, never
客观真理永远用一般现在时：The earth goes round the sun.
examples: She goes to school by bus every day.
Light travels faster than sound.
common_mistakes: She go → She goes（三单加s）
He doesn't goes → He doesn't go（doesn't后原形）

rules: 一般过去时：表示过去发生的动作或状态
规则变化：动词+ed（以e结尾+d；辅音+y变i+ed；重读闭音节双写+ed）
不规则变化需要单独记忆：go→went, come→came, see→saw, take→took, get→got, make→made, have→had, do→did, say→said, tell→told
标志词：yesterday, last night/week/month/year, ...ago, just now, in 1990, the other day
examples: I saw a great movie last night.
He came to China in 2010.
common_mistakes: I see him yesterday → I saw him yesterday
He didn't went → He didn't go（did 后原形）

rules: 一般将来时：will/shall + 动词原形
be going to + 动词原形 = 打算做/即将发生
标志词：tomorrow, next week/month/year, in the future, soon, later
时间/条件状语从句中用一般现在时表将来（主将从现）
过去将来时：would + 动词原形（用于宾语从句中，主句为过去时）
examples: They will arrive tomorrow.
He said he would come the next day.
common_mistakes: He will comes → He will come（will 后原形）
If it will rain → If it rains（从句用现在时）

rules: 现在完成时：have/has + 过去分词
用法：①过去动作对现在的影响；②从过去持续到现在的动作/状态
标志词：since+时间点, for+时间段, already, yet, ever, never, just, recently, so far, up to now, in the past few years
have been to（去过已回）, have gone to（去了未回）, have been in（待在某地）
examples: She has lived here since 2010.
I have already finished my homework.
common_mistakes: I have gone to Beijing → I have been to Beijing（已回）
for 和 since 混淆

rules: 过去完成时：had + 过去分词（过去的过去）
用于表示在过去某个时间或动作之前已经完成的动作
标志词：by the end of last..., by the time + 过去时, before + 过去时
常用于宾语从句中，主句为过去时，从句动作发生在主句动作之前
hardly/scarcely...when；no sooner...than 句型中主句用过去完成时
examples: By the end of last month, we had learned 2000 words.
He had left before I arrived.
common_mistakes: 混淆现在完成时和过去完成时
had 后不用过去式：had went → had gone

rules: 进行时态：be + 现在分词（-ing）
现在进行时：am/is/are + doing——表示此刻正在进行的动作
过去进行时：was/were + doing——表示过去某时刻正在进行的动作
标志词：now, right now, at the moment, Look!, Listen!
while 从句常用过去进行时
现在分词变化：①一般+ing；②不发音e结尾去e+ing；③重读闭音节双写+ing；④ie变y+ing
examples: Look! The birds are flying in the sky.
While I was cooking, the phone rang.
common_mistakes: He is run → He is running（双写n）
表状态和心理的动词不用进行时：know, want, like, believe

rules: 被动语态：be + 过去分词
各种时态的被动：一般现在→am/is/are+done；一般过去→was/were+done；一般将来→will be+done；现在完成→have/has been+done；情态动词→can/must be+done
被动语态的使用：不知道/不必知道动作执行者；强调动作承受者
主动变被动：宾语变主语，谓语变 be+done，主语变 by+宾语
感官动词/使役动词在被动中要还原 to：make sb do → sb be made to do
examples: English is spoken all over the world.
The window was broken by Tom.
common_mistakes: The cake was made by my mother yesterday ✅
He was made work → He was made to work（被动还原 to）

rules: 情态动词后接动词原形，无人称和数的变化
can/could = 能够/可以（能力/允许）
may/might = 可以/可能（允许/推测）
must = 必须/一定（义务/强制/推测）。mustn't = 禁止/不准
should/ought to = 应该（建议/义务）
need = 需要（情态动词用法：need do；实义动词：need to do）。needn't = 不必
will/would = 将/愿意。would 也用于委婉请求
had better + 原形 = 最好做。否定：had better not do
used to do = 过去常常。be used to doing = 习惯做
examples: You mustn't smoke here.
He used to live in London.
Must I go? No, you needn't.
common_mistakes: mustn't = 禁止(不是"不必")
had better to do → had better do
used to do 和 be used to doing 混淆

rules: 主谓一致核心规则：谓语动词在数和人称上与主语保持一致
就近原则：either...or, neither...nor, not only...but also, there be
意义一致：集合名词、学科名称、时间距离金钱
语法一致：不定式/动名词/主语从句→单数
many a + 单数名词 → 单数
a number of + 复数名词 → 复数（=many）
the number of + 复数名词 → 单数（...的数量）
more than one + 单数名词 → 单数
examples: Neither he nor I am wrong.
A number of students are absent today.
The number of students is 50.
common_mistakes: A number of... is → are
The number of... are → is

rules: 动词短语 = 动词 + 介词/副词，构成新的含义

常见动词短语：look for(寻找), look after(照顾), look up(查阅), look forward to(期待)；turn on(打开), turn off(关闭), turn up(调高), turn down(调低/拒绝)；take off(起飞/脱下), take up(开始从事), take care of(照顾)；put on(穿上), put off(推迟), put up(张贴)；give up(放弃), give in(屈服), give away(赠送)；get up(起床), get on(上车), get off(下车), get along with(相处)
代词宾语必须放在动词和副词之间：turn it on ✅，turn on it ❌
examples: Please turn off the light. / Please turn it off.
He gave up smoking last year.
common_mistakes: turn on it → turn it on（代词放中间）

rules: 不定式 to do 可以作宾语。下列动词只能接不定式作宾语：want, decide, promise, agree, refuse, plan, hope, wish, expect, learn, manage, offer, prepare, pretend, afford, fail, happen, seem
疑问词 + to do：what to do, how to do it, where to go, when to start
it 作形式宾语：find/think/feel/make + it + adj + to do
examples: He promised to come on time.
I don't know what to do next.
common_mistakes: want doing → want to do
decide doing → decide to do

sub_modules.name: 不定式作主语、状语与定语

rules: 不定式作主语：To learn English well is important. → 常用 It is important to learn English well.
不定式作定语：I have something to tell you. / a house to live in
不定式作状语：表目的(in order to/so as to), 表结果(too...to, enough to), 表原因
It is + adj + for/of sb to do：for 表客观情况，of 表人品特征
examples: It is necessary for you to take exercise.
He is too young to go to school.
common_mistakes: a pen to write → a pen to write with（介词不能省）

rules: 动名词 doing 作宾语。下列动词只能接动名词：enjoy, mind, finish, practise, keep, avoid, suggest, admit, deny, miss, imagine, consider, risk, delay, postpone, appreciate
介词 + 动名词：thank you for helping, be good at doing, be interested in doing
注意：look forward to/be used to/devote to 中的 to 是介词，后接 doing
stop/remember/forget/regret/try/mean/go on + doing vs to do 含义不同
examples: I enjoy reading books.
Would you mind opening the window?
common_mistakes: enjoy to do → enjoy doing
look forward to see → look forward to seeing

rules: 动名词作主语表示一般性、习惯性的行为：Reading is my hobby.
It is no use/no good/useless/worth + doing
There is no + doing = 不可能...：There is no knowing what will happen.
动名词的复合结构：物主代词/名词所有格 + doing：His coming surprised us.
examples: Swimming is good for health.
It is no use crying over spilt milk.
common_mistakes: Read is my hobby → Reading is my hobby（动名词作主语）

rules: 现在分词(-ing)表示主动或正在进行的动作
现在分词作定语：the sleeping baby = the baby who is sleeping
现在分词作状语：Hearing the news, he jumped with joy.
现在分词作宾补：see/hear/watch sb doing
现在分词的完成式：having done（表示发生在谓语之前）
examples: The girl standing there is my sister.
Having finished his homework, he went out to play.
common_mistakes: Having finished 和 Having been finished 混淆

rules: 过去分词(-ed)表示被动或已完成的动作
过去分词作定语：the broken window = the window which was broken
过去分词作状语：Seen from the hill, the city looks beautiful.
过去分词作宾补：have/get sth done, make oneself understood
不规则动词的过去分词需要单独记忆
examples: The book written by Lu Xun is very popular.
I had my hair cut yesterday.
common_mistakes: 现在分词和过去分词混淆：falling leaves(正在落的) vs fallen leaves(已落下的)

sub_modules.name: 非谓语动词易混淆辨析

rules: 不定式 vs 动名词：1) 只能接不定式：want, decide, promise；2) 只能接动名词：enjoy, mind, finish；3) 两者均可但含义不同：stop, remember, forget, regret, try, mean
stop to do(停下来去做) vs stop doing(停止做)
remember to do(记得要做) vs remember doing(记得做过)
forget to do(忘记要做) vs forget doing(忘记做过)
regret to do(遗憾要做) vs regret doing(后悔做过)
try to do(努力做) vs try doing(尝试做)
mean to do(打算做) vs mean doing(意味着)
examples: Remember to lock the door. / I remember locking the door.
He tried to open the door but failed. / He tried knocking at the back door.
common_mistakes: stop to do 和 stop doing 含义混淆
forget/remember 的 to do/doing 区分

rules: 非谓语动词不能单独作谓语。选择哪一种非谓语形式取决于：①与逻辑主语的关系（主动/被动）；②与谓语动词的时间关系（同时/之前）
doing = 主动/同时；having done = 主动/之前；done = 被动/完成；being done = 被动/同时；having been done = 被动/之前
to do = 将来/目的；to be doing = 同时进行；to have done = 之前完成
examples: Having finished his work, he went home.
The building being built now is a hospital.
common_mistakes: Having finished vs Having been finished 混淆

rules: 英语五大基本句型：①S+V（主谓）：Birds fly.；②S+V+O（主谓宾）：I love you.；③S+V+P（主系表）：She is happy.；④S+V+IO+DO（主谓间宾直宾）：He gave me a book.；⑤S+V+O+OC（主谓宾宾补）：We made him monitor.
简单句→并列句→复合句
陈述句、疑问句、祈使句、感叹句四种句子类型
examples: She gave me a present.（S+V+IO+DO）
They painted the wall white.（S+V+O+OC）
common_mistakes: 缺少谓语动词
一个简单句中出现两个谓语动词

rules: There be 句型表示"某地存在某人/物"
be 动词与最靠近的名词保持数的一致（就近原则）
There be 的各种时态：There is/are, There was/were, There will be, There have/has been, There used to be
There be + 名词 + doing/done/to do：There is a man waiting outside.
There seems/appears/happens to be...
examples: There is a book and two pens on the desk.
There used to be a cinema here.
common_mistakes: There have a book → There is a book（不能用 have）
There are a book → There is a book（就近 a book）

rules: 反意疑问句 = 陈述句 + 简短问句。前肯后否，前否后肯
时态和主语要与陈述部分一致
Let's... → shall we?；Let us... → will you?
祈使句 → will you? / won't you?
I'm... → aren't I?
I think/believe + 从句 → 反意与从句一致
陈述部分有否定词(never, seldom, hardly等)视为否定，反意用肯定
examples: He is a student, isn't he?
Let's go, shall we?
I don't think he will come, will he?
common_mistakes: I'm right, amn't I → aren't I
Let's 和 Let us 的反意不同

rules: 感叹句：What + (a/an) + 形容词 + 名词 + 主谓！；How + 形容词/副词 + 主谓！
What 修饰名词；How 修饰形容词或副词
What a nice day (it is)! = How nice a day it is!
What + 不可数名词/复数名词！：What nice weather!
examples: What a beautiful flower!
How fast he runs!
common_mistakes: What fast he runs → How fast he runs（副词用 How）
How a beautiful flower → What a beautiful flower（名词用 What）

rules: 否定句的构成：be/have/情态动词+not；实义动词用 don't/doesn't/didn't
部分否定：not all, not every, not both = 并非所有的都
双重否定 = 肯定：You can't not go.（你必须去）
否定转移：I don't think he will come. = I think he won't come.
半否定词：hardly, scarcely, rarely, seldom, little, few
examples: I don't think it will rain.
Not all the students passed the exam.
common_mistakes: I think he won't come → I don't think he will come（否定转移）

rules: 完全倒装：Here/There/Now/Then/Out/In + 谓语 + 主语
部分倒装：否定词/Only+状语在句首时，主句部分倒装（助动词/情态动词+主语+动词）
常用部分倒装的情况：Never/Seldom/Hardly/Scarcely/Not only/No sooner/Not until/Only then
So/Neither/Nor + 助动词 + 主语 = ...也一样/也不
as/though 引导的让步从句：Child as he is, he knows a lot.
examples: Never have I seen such a beautiful place.
Only in this way can we solve the problem.
common_mistakes: Only then he realized → Only then did he realize
Hardly he had → Hardly had he

rules: 强调句型：It is/was + 被强调部分 + that/who + 其余
可以强调主语、宾语、状语，但不能强调谓语
强调谓语：do/does/did + 动词原形
What...is/was... 结构也可表强调
examples: It was Tom who broke the window.
I do hope you can come.
common_mistakes: 强调时间/地点时误用 when/where 代替 that

rules: that 引导的宾语从句：that 只起连接作用，在从句中不充当成分，口语中可省略
常用 that 引导宾语从句的动词：say, think, believe, know, hope, wish, suggest, find, hear, see, notice, explain, show, prove
that 从句作介词的宾语：except that, in that, but that
it 作形式宾语：I think it necessary that...
suggest/insist/order/demand + that + (should) do（虚拟语气）
examples: I think (that) he is right.
He suggested that we (should) start early.
common_mistakes: 漏掉 that（正式文体需保留）
suggest 后的从句忘记用虚拟语气

rules: whether/if 引导的宾语从句，表示"是否"
whether 和 if 的区别：①whether 可用于介词后（It depends on whether...）；②whether 可用于不定式前（I don't know whether to go）；③whether 可与 or not 直接连用（whether or not）；④whether 可引导主语从句（Whether he comes is uncertain）
if 不能用于以上四种情况
whether...or = 无论...还是...
examples: I don't know whether/if he will come.
It depends on whether it rains.
common_mistakes: 介词后用 if 代替 whether
whether/if 引导的从句语序错误

sub_modules.name: 疑问词引导的宾语从句

rules: wh- 词引导的宾语从句：what, who, whom, which, whose, where, when, why, how
关键规则：从句必须用陈述语序（主语+谓语），不能用疑问语序
比较：Do you know where he lives?（陈述语序✅）→ where does he live?（疑问语序❌）
what 在从句中作主语、宾语或表语：I don't know what happened.
疑问词 + to do：I don't know what to do.
examples: Can you tell me where the post office is?
I want to know when we should start.
common_mistakes: Can you tell me where is the post office? → ...where the post office is?（陈述语序）

rules: 宾语从句的时态呼应：主句为现在时→从句可用任何时态；主句为过去时→从句必须用相应的过去时
客观真理永远用一般现在时，不受主句时态影响：He said the earth goes round the sun.
主句为过去时，从句时态变化：一般现在→一般过去；现在进行→过去进行；现在完成→过去完成；一般将来→过去将来
examples: He said he would come the next day.
She told me she had finished her work.
common_mistakes: 主句过去时，从句用现在时（客观真理除外）

rules: 时间状语从句的时态：主将从现
when/while/as 的区别：when 广泛使用；while 加持续动作；as 加短暂动作或表示"随着"
as soon as/the moment/the minute = 一...就
until/till：肯定句（持续到...为止）；not...until（直到...才）
since：主句用现在完成时，从句用一般过去时
before/after：before 从句用一般现在时表将来；after 从句可用现在完成时强调动作完成
examples: I will call you as soon as I arrive.
He didn't go to bed until his mother came back.
common_mistakes: When he will come → When he comes
until 和 not...until 混淆

rules: 条件状语从句：主将从现——主句用将来时，if/unless 从句用一般现在时
if = 如果：If it rains, we will stay home.
unless = if...not = 除非/如果不：You will fail unless you study hard.
as long as/so long as = 只要
in case = 以防/万一
on condition that/provided that = 条件是
examples: If you work hard, you will succeed.
I won't go unless I am invited.
common_mistakes: If it will rain → If it rains
unless 误用为 if

rules: 让步状语从句：although/though（虽然）, even though/even if（即使）, while/whereas（虽然/然而）, no matter + wh-（无论）
关键规则：although/though 不能与 but 同时使用
as/though 倒装：Child as he is, he knows a lot.
whether...or = 无论...还是
examples: Although he is old, he is very active.
No matter what happens, I will support you.
common_mistakes: Although...but 连用
no matter what 与 whatever 的用法区别

rules: 原因状语从句：because（因为，语气最强，回答 why）, since（既然）, as（由于）, for（因为，并列连词）
because 和 so 不能同时使用
because of + 名词、due to + 名词、thanks to + 名词、owing to + 名词
now that = 既然
examples: He was late because he missed the bus.
Since everyone is here, let's begin.
common_mistakes: because...so 连用
because 和 because of 混淆

rules: 目的状语从句：so that/in order that + 从句（从句常用 can/could/may/might）
结果状语从句：so...that/such...that = 如此...以至于
so + adj/adv + that；such + (a/an) + adj + n + that
so that 既可表目的也可表结果
in order to/so as to + 动词原形（简单句中的目的状语）
examples: He got up early so that he could catch the first bus.
It is such a good book that I have read it three times.
common_mistakes: so...that 和 such...that 混淆
so as to 的否定：so as not to

rules: 关系代词引导定语从句：who(人/主), whom(人/宾), which(物), that(通用), whose(所属)
who/whom/which 可用 that 替换（在限制性定语从句中）
关系代词在从句中作宾语时可以省略：The man (who/that) I saw was Tom.
介词 + which/whom：The house in which he lives is old.
只用 that 不用 which 的情况：①先行词为不定代词(all, much, little等)；②先行词有最高级/序数词修饰；③先行词有人又有物
examples: The man who is talking to Mary is my uncle.
This is the best film that I have ever seen.
common_mistakes: who 和 whom 混淆
that 用于非限制性定语从句

rules: 必须用 that 的情况：①先行词为 all, everything, nothing, something, anything, little, much 等不定代词；②先行词被序数词或最高级修饰；③先行词被 the only, the very, the same, the last 修饰；④先行词既有人又有物；⑤主句以 who/which 开头的疑问句
不能用 that 的情况：①非限制性定语从句（that 只能用于限制性定语从句）；②介词后（介词+which/whom，不用 that）
examples: All that glitters is not gold.
He is the only person that can help you.
common_mistakes: 在非限制性定语从句中用 that
介词后用 that

rules: 关系副词引导定语从句：when(时间), where(地点), why(原因)
when 的先行词：time, day, year, moment 等
where 的先行词：place, house, city, country, school 等
why 的先行词：reason
关系副词 = 介词 + which：when = at/in/on which; where = at/in which; why = for which
examples: I will never forget the day when I met her.
This is the reason why I was late.
common_mistakes: 关系副词和关系代词混淆
when 和 that 混用

sub_modules.name: 限制性vs非限制性定语从句

rules: 限制性定语从句：对先行词起限定作用，不可省略，不用逗号隔开
非限制性定语从句：对先行词起补充说明作用，可省略，用逗号隔开，用 which/who，不用 that
as 引导的定语从句：as is known to all, as we all know
"介词+关系代词" 结构：in which, for which, of whom
examples: He has two sons who are doctors.（可能还有别的儿子——限制性）
He has two sons, who are doctors.（只有两个儿子——非限制性）
common_mistakes: 非限制性定语从句用 that

rules: if 条件句的虚拟语气：与现在事实相反→If I were you, I would go.；与过去事实相反→If I had known, I would have told you.；与将来事实相反→If it were to rain, we would stay home.
错综时间条件句：If you had followed my advice, you would be fine now.
含蓄条件句：without/but for/otherwise
if only = 要是...就好了（虚拟语气）
as if/as though + 虚拟语气
examples: If I were you, I would accept the offer.
If he had come earlier, he would have met her.
common_mistakes: If I was you → If I were you
时间混乱：主从句时态不匹配

rules: 名词性从句中的虚拟语气：suggest, order, demand, propose, insist, recommend, require, request + that + (should) + 动词原形
It is necessary/important/strange that + (should) do
wish + 过去时（对现在虚拟）/ 过去完成时（对过去虚拟）/ would do（对将来愿望）
would rather + 过去时（对现在/将来虚拟）/ 过去完成时（对过去虚拟）
It is (high) time that + 过去时 = 该是做...的时候了
examples: I suggest that he (should) go there at once.
I wish I had listened to your advice.
common_mistakes: suggest 后不用虚拟语气
wish 后的时态用错

rules: It is (high/about) time that + 过去时/should do
would rather + sb + 过去时
as if/as though + 虚拟语气
if only + 虚拟语气
lest/for fear that + (should) do
含蓄条件：without/but for/or/otherwise
examples: It is high time that we went to bed.
I would rather you came tomorrow.
common_mistakes: It is time that + 现在时

rules: 问候：How do you do?（初次见面，回答也是 How do you do?）
How are you?（熟人问候，回答 Fine, thank you.）
Nice to meet you.（回答 Nice to meet you, too.）
Good morning/afternoon/evening!
How is everything going?
examples: — How do you do? — How do you do?
— Nice to meet you. — Nice to meet you, too.
common_mistakes: How do you do? 回答 I'm fine.→ How do you do?（初次见面固定回答）

rules: 请求允许：Can/Could/May I...?；Would/Do you mind if I...?
请求帮助：Can/Could/Would you...?；Would you mind doing...?
回答允许：Sure./Of course./Certainly./Go ahead.
回答拒绝：Sorry, I'm afraid not./I'd love to, but...
Would you mind...? 回答：不介意→Not at all./Of course not.；介意→Sorry./I'm afraid...
examples: — Would you mind opening the window? — Not at all.
— May I use your phone? — Sure. Go ahead.
common_mistakes: Would you mind...? 回答 Yes 表示介意（错）
Can/Could/May 在正式程度上的区别

rules: 发出邀请：Would you like to...? / I'd like to invite you to...
接受邀请：Yes, I'd love to./That sounds great./It's very kind of you.
拒绝邀请：I'd love to, but.../Sorry, I'm afraid I can't.
Would you like some...? 肯定回答→Yes, please.；否定→No, thanks.
examples: — Would you like to join us? — I'd love to.
— Shall we go swimming? — Good idea!
common_mistakes: Would you like...? 回答 Yes, I like → Yes, I'd love to

rules: 表示同意：I agree with you./I think so, too./That's right./Exactly.
表示不同意：I don't think so./I'm afraid I disagree./I'm afraid you're wrong.
So do I./Neither do I. 的用法：So+助动词+主语=...也一样；Neither/Nor+助动词+主语=...也不
examples: — I like music. — So do I.
— I can't swim. — Neither can I.
common_mistakes: So do I 和 So I do 混淆
Neither 后用错助动词

rules: 感谢：Thank you./Thanks a lot. 回答：You're welcome./My pleasure./Not at all./Don't mention it.
道歉：I'm sorry./Excuse me. 回答：That's all right./Never mind./It doesn't matter.
祝愿：Good luck!/Have a nice day! 回答：Thank you./The same to you.
打电话：Hello, this is...speaking./May I speak to...?/Hold on, please.
购物：Can I help you?/What can I do for you?/How much is it?
问路：Excuse me, where is...?/How can I get to...?
看病：What's wrong with you?/I have a headache./Take this medicine.
examples: — Thank you very much. — You're welcome.
— I'm sorry I'm late. — That's all right.
common_mistakes: Thank you 回答用 No thanks（错）
Excuse me 和 I'm sorry 混淆
